canonical_question: How do I design global DNS and load balancing?
aliases: GeoDNS vs Anycast vs GSLB comparison
design global server load balancing system
how to implement global traffic management
multi-region load balancing with DNS failover
Route 53 latency routing vs Cloudflare load balancing

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Cloudflare adaptive load balancing GA (2024), Route 53 Application Recovery Controller (2023)

constraints: DNS TTL floor: Never set TTL below 30s in production
Anycast requires a /24 IPv4 or /48 IPv6 prefix minimum
Health checks must run from multiple vantage points (minimum 3 regions)
DNS-only load balancing cannot enforce session affinity
GeoDNS accuracy degrades with VPN/proxy users and ECS non-compliance

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs L4/L7 load balancing within a single datacenter
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Local load balancer configuration guides (NGINX/HAProxy upstream docs)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs CDN configuration, not DNS-level traffic routing
User needs internal service mesh routing (Envoy, Istio, Linkerd)

inputs_needed.question: What is your primary routing goal?
What is your expected global request volume?
Where are your backend servers deployed?
